properties of the Abs_pq constructor and the alist_of function, particularly the inverse relationship between ....<br/>Step7: Distinctness and the Minimum Element:<br/>	Since the alist_of q list is distinct and sorted, the first element hd (alist_of q) is not in the tail of the list tl (alist_of q).<br/>	Therefore, the first component of the head of the list, fst (hd (alist_of q)), is not in the p.font:
You are an expert in theorem proving in isabelle. your task is to provide formal proof in response to agiven problem statement
and informal .Your proof should be structured and clearly written, meeting the following criteria: - It can be verified by Isabelle.
- Each step of the proof should be explained in detail using comments enclosed in ”(*” and ”*)”. - The explanation for each
step should be clear and concise, avoiding any unnecessary or apologetic language.

p.font:
Theorem: Given pop q = Some (v, q1), we want to show that v \<notin> set (values q1).
Step1: Case Analysis on is_empty q:
Step2: Case 1: Assume is_empty q = True.
In this case, by the definition of pop, pop q should be None. This contradicts our assumption that pop q = Some (v, q1)..
Step3: Case 2: Assume is_empty q = False.
Here, by the definition of pop, we have pop q = Some (min q, remove_min q). Therefore, from our assumption, it follows ...
Step4: Unfold the values Function for q1: Using the definition of values, we have: values (q1) = values (remove_min q) = ...
Step5: By the properties of the Abs_pq constructor and the alist_of function, particularly the inverse relationship between ....
Step7: Distinctness and the Minimum Element:
Since the alist_of q list is distinct and sorted, the first element hd (alist_of q) is not in the tail of the list tl (alist_of q).
Therefore, the first component of the head of the list, fst (hd (alist_of q)), is not in the set set (map fst (tl (alist_of q))). ..
Step8: Conclusion: Combining the above results, we conclude that v \<notin> set (values q1), which completes the proof.
